-# Blue Mountain Friends Scraper - Setup Complete ✅
-
-## Summary
-
-I've created a scraper for **Friends of Blue Mountain-Birch Cove Lakes** hiking and outdoor activities!
-
-## About the Organization
-
-The Friends of Blue Mountain-Birch Cove Lakes Society advocates for North America's largest urban wilderness park - a 1,700+ hectare protected area in Halifax. They organize guided hikes, nature walks, and community events throughout the Blue Mountain-Birch Cove Lakes Wilderness Area.
-
-## What Was Created
-
-### 1. Main Scraper File
-**Location:** &#96;scrapers/blue_mountain_friends_scraper.py&#96;
-
-**Features:**
-- Uses Playwright to handle slow-loading pages
-- Flexible parsing: handles both list pages and individual event pages
-- Multiple parsing strategies to extract events from different HTML structures
-- Extracts: title, date, time, description, cost, images, venue
-- Outputs to: &#96;output/blue_mountain_friends_events.csv&#96;
-
-**Special Capabilities:**
-- Handles the site's slow load times with extended timeouts (90 seconds)
-- Can parse multiple events from a single activities page
-- Falls back to parsing the main activities page if no separate event URLs exist
-- Looks for events in articles, divs, sections, and list items
-
-### 2. Registry Entry
-**File:** &#96;scraper_registry.py&#96;
-
-&#96;&#96;&#96;python
-ScraperConfig(
-    key="bluemountainfriends",
-    name="Blue Mountain Friends",
-    script=rel("scrapers", "blue_mountain_friends_scraper.py"),
-    output=rel("output", "blue_mountain_friends_events.csv"),
-    enabled=True,
-    notes="Hiking and wilderness activities at Blue Mountain-Birch Cove Lakes",
-)
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-## How to Use
-
-### Run the Blue Mountain Friends scraper alone:
-&#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-cd /path/to/halifax_event_scrapers_v3
-python3 scrapers/blue_mountain_friends_scraper.py
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-### Run all scrapers (including Blue Mountain Friends):
-&#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-python3 master_runner.py
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-The scraper is **already enabled** and will run automatically!
-
-## Event Details
-
-**Source:** https://bluemountainfriends.ca/activities/#hiking
-**Organizer:** Friends of Blue Mountain-Birch Cove Lakes
-**Venue:** Blue Mountain-Birch Cove Lakes Wilderness Area
-
-**Event Categories:**
-- Outdoors &amp; Nature (all events)
-- Sports &amp; Recreation (all events)
-- Family &amp; Kids (auto-detected if mentioned)
-- Workshops &amp; Classes (auto-detected if mentioned)
-
-**Tags:** "blue mountain, birch cove lakes, hiking, trails, wilderness, halifax, outdoors"
-
-## Technical Details
-
-### Why This Scraper is Different
-
-The Blue Mountain Friends website presented unique challenges:
-1. **Slow Loading:** Page takes 30+ seconds to fully load
-2. **Blocking:** Site blocks automated requests (403 errors)
-3. **Structure Unknown:** Events may be on separate pages or listed on one page
-
-**Solutions Implemented:**
-- Extended timeouts (90 seconds for navigation)
-- Multiple parsing strategies (articles, divs, list items)
-- Graceful handling of load failures
-- Can parse events from listing page or individual event pages
-
-### Parsing Strategies
-
-The scraper tries multiple approaches:
-1. **Strategy 1:** Look for &#96;&lt;article&gt;&#96; elements
-2. **Strategy 2:** Find divs/sections with "event", "activity", "hike" in class names
-3. **Strategy 3:** Parse list items &#96;&lt;li&gt;&#96; that contain dates
-4. **Fallback:** If no separate event links found, parse activities from main page
-
-### Date Detection
-
-Only includes events with valid dates. Looks for:
-- Numeric patterns: &#96;12/25/2026&#96;, &#96;12-25-26&#96;
-- Text patterns: &#96;January 15&#96;, &#96;Feb 14, 2026&#96;
-- Full date strings: &#96;Saturday, February 14, 2026&#96;
-
-## Testing Notes
-
-During development:
-- The site was very slow to load (timeout issues)
-- WebFetch returned 403 errors (blocking automated access)
-- Browser tools also timed out due to slow page loads
-
-**The scraper is designed to handle these issues** with:
-- Extended timeouts
-- Retry-friendly architecture
-- Multiple parsing fallbacks
-
-## About the Wilderness Area
-
-Blue Mountain-Birch Cove Lakes is:
-- 1,700+ hectares of protected wilderness
-- Located in Halifax (Hammonds Plains to Bayers Lake area)
-- Features multiple hiking trails, lakes, and lookout points
-- Proposed as North America's largest urban wilderness park
-
-Activities typically include:
-- Guided nature walks
-- Trail maintenance events
-- Community hikes
-- Educational programs
-- Conservation activities
-
-## Troubleshooting
-
-If the scraper encounters issues:
-
-**No events found:**
-- Site may have no current events listed
-- Check if activities are posted on social media instead
-- Site structure may have changed (update parsing strategies)
-
-**Timeout errors:**
-- Increase timeout values in the code
-- Check if site is down/slow
-- Try running during off-peak hours
-
-**403 errors:**
-- Playwright should bypass this, but site may have enhanced blocking
-- Check if site has new anti-bot measures
-
-## Related Organizations
-
-Consider also scraping from:
-- Halifax Northwest Trails (halifaxnorthwesttrails.ca)
-- Halifax Trails (halifaxtrails.ca)
-- Ecology Action Centre wilderness programs
-
----
-
-**Created:** January 29, 2026
-**Source:** https://bluemountainfriends.ca/activities/#hiking
-**Status:** ✅ Created &amp; Registered
-**Format:** TEC-compatible CSV
-
-**References:**
-- [Halifax Trails - Blue Mountain Area](https://www.halifaxtrails.ca/kearney-lake-trail-system/)
-- [Nova Scotia Nature Trust - Blue Mountain](https://nsnt.ca/blog/bmbcl/)
-- [Ecology Action Centre - Blue Mountain-Birch Cove Lakes](https://ecologyaction.ca/our-work/wilderness/blue-mountain-birch-coves-lakes)
diff --git a/CARBONARC_FIX_SUMMARY.md b/CARBONARC_FIX_SUMMARY.md
deleted file mode 100644
index 4915e6e..0000000
--- a/CARBONARC_FIX_SUMMARY.md
+++ /dev/null
@@ -1,129 +0,0 @@
-# Carbon Arc Scraper Fixes - Summary
-
-## 🐛 Original Issues
-
-1. **Wrong Image Being Scraped**: The scraper was grabbing the Carbon Arc logo instead of actual movie posters
-2. **YouTube Videos Not Handled**: Events with embedded video trailers had no images extracted
-3. **CSV Import Concatenation**: The cost value was being appended to the image URL during WordPress import (e.g., &#96;...?format=1500w12&#96;)
-
-## ✅ Changes Made
-
-### 1. Added Default Image Support (&#96;default_images.py&#96;)
-- Added Carbon Arc logo as fallback: &#96;"carbonarc": "https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/..."&#96;
-- This logo will only be used when no other image is found
-
-### 2. Smart Image Extraction (&#96;carbonarc_scraper.py&#96;)
-Created two new helper functions:
-
-#### &#96;extract_youtube_thumbnail()&#96;
-- Detects embedded YouTube videos on event pages
-- Extracts video ID from iframe src
-- Returns high-quality thumbnail: &#96;https://img.youtube.com/vi/{video_id}/maxresdefault.jpg&#96;
-
-#### &#96;extract_event_poster()&#96; - 4-Tier Fallback Strategy
-1. **YouTube Thumbnail** (priority #1) - For events with video trailers
-2. **Actual Poster Images** - Searches for real event images, skipping:
-   - Carbon Arc logo (&#96;carbon_arc_1920x1080&#96;)
-   - Small images (&lt; 100x100px) - likely icons/buttons
-3. **og:image Meta Tag** - From page metadata (if not the logo)
-4. **Default Logo** - Falls back to Carbon Arc logo only if nothing else found
-
-### 3. Improved CSV Formatting
-Updated &#96;write_csv()&#96; function to use:
-- &#96;quoting=csv.QUOTE_NONNUMERIC&#96; - Quotes all non-numeric fields
-- &#96;escapechar='\\'&#96; - Proper escape handling
-- This ensures better compatibility with The Events Calendar WordPress plugin
-
-## 🧪 Testing Instructions
-
-### When You Have Network Access:
-
-1. **Run the scraper:**
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-   cd /path/to/halifax_event_scrapers_v3
-   python3 scrapers/carbonarc_scraper.py
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-2. **Check the output:**
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-   # View a few events
-   head -5 output/carbonarc_events.csv | cut -d',' -f19
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-3. **Look for:**
-   - ✅ YouTube thumbnail URLs for video-based events: &#96;https://img.youtube.com/vi/...&#96;
-   - ✅ Actual movie poster images (not always the same Carbon Arc logo)
-   - ✅ Carbon Arc logo only appears as fallback when no poster exists
-
-### After WordPress Import:
-
-Visit an event page like: https://halifax-now.ca/event/peter-hujars-day-carbon-arc/
-
-**Expected Results:**
-- ✅ Featured image should display the YouTube thumbnail (or actual poster if available)
-- ✅ Cost field should show just the price (e.g., "12" or "10.60")
-- ✅ NO concatenation between image URL and cost
-
-## 📝 What Changed in the Code
-
-### Files Modified:
-1. &#96;scrapers/default_images.py&#96; - Added Carbon Arc logo
-2. &#96;scrapers/carbonarc_scraper.py&#96; - Added 3 new features:
-   - &#96;extract_youtube_thumbnail()&#96; function
-   - &#96;extract_event_poster()&#96; function
-   - Improved CSV quoting in &#96;write_csv()&#96;
-
-### Lines of Code:
-- Added: ~75 lines (new image extraction logic)
-- Modified: ~10 lines (CSV writer, import statement)
-
-## 🎯 Expected Behavior
-
-### Events WITH Movie Posters:
-- Image: Actual poster from carbonarc.ca
-- Cost: Price only (e.g., "$12")
-
-### Events WITH YouTube Videos:
-- Image: YouTube video thumbnail
-- Cost: Price only
-
-### Events WITHOUT Images:
-- Image: Carbon Arc logo (fallback)
-- Cost: Price only
-
-## 🔍 Troubleshooting
-
-If issues persist after running the updated scraper:
-
-1. **Check if new CSV has proper quoting:**
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-   head -2 output/carbonarc_events.csv
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;
-   URLs and text should be in quotes.
-
-2. **Verify The Events Calendar plugin settings:**
-   - Check CSV import field mapping
-   - Ensure "Featured Image" field maps correctly
-   - Try re-importing with the new CSV
-
-3. **Clear WordPress cache** after re-importing events
-
-## 📊 Before vs After
-
-### BEFORE:
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-Featured Image: carbon_arc_1920x1080_...png?format=1500w12  ❌
-Cost: (empty or corrupted)
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-### AFTER:
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-Featured Image: https://img.youtube.com/vi/VIDEO_ID/maxresdefault.jpg  ✅
-Cost: 12  ✅
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
----
-
-**Created:** February 11, 2026
-**Issue:** Squarespace image URL appearing in wrong fields
-**Status:** Fixed and ready for testing
diff --git a/CLEANUP_README.md b/CLEANUP_README.md
deleted file mode 100644
index 20ee512..0000000
--- a/CLEANUP_README.md
+++ /dev/null
@@ -1,107 +0,0 @@
-# Automated Cleanup System
-
-This folder contains scripts to automatically clean up old temporary files from your Halifax event scraper project.
-
-## What Gets Cleaned Up?
-
-### CSV Files (older than 45 days)
-- &#96;audit_*.csv&#96; - Old audit reports
-- &#96;ready_to_import_*.csv&#96; - Old import files
-- &#96;*_audit.csv&#96; - Audit files
-- &#96;event_*.csv&#96; - Temporary event files
-
-### Protected Files (NEVER deleted)
-- &#96;master_events.csv&#96; - Your main data file
-- Any file with &#96;_master.csv&#96; in the name
-- Files in &#96;venv/&#96;, &#96;__pycache__/&#96;, &#96;.git/&#96;
-- Master files in &#96;output/&#96; or &#96;scrapers/&#96; directories
-
-### Event Images (older than 45 days)
-- Images in your &#96;~/Downloads/&#96; folder
-- Only deletes images with "event", "halifax", or "venue" in the filename
-- Formats: .jpg, .jpeg, .png, .gif, .webp
-
-## How to Use
-
-### Option 1: Test Run (Dry Run)
-See what would be deleted without actually deleting anything:
-
-&#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-python3 cleanup_old_files.py --dry-run
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-### Option 2: Manual Cleanup
-Run the cleanup manually whenever you want:
-
-&#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-python3 cleanup_old_files.py
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-### Option 3: Automatic Monthly Cleanup
-The cleanup is configured to run automatically on the 1st of each month at 2:00 AM using the Claude shortcut system.
-
-## Cleanup Reports
-
-Every time the cleanup runs, it generates a report in the &#96;cleanup_reports/&#96; folder:
-- Shows how many files were deleted
-- Lists what was removed (if under 50 files)
-- Reports total space freed
-
-Reports are named: &#96;cleanup_YYYY-MM-DD.txt&#96;
-
-## Safety Features
-
-1. **Dry run first**: If more than 100 files would be deleted, the script automatically runs in dry-run mode
-2. **Protected patterns**: Core data files are never touched
-3. **Age verification**: Only files older than 45 days are removed
-4. **Detailed logging**: Every cleanup generates a report
-
-## Customization
-
-To change the settings, edit the top of &#96;cleanup_old_files.py&#96;:
-
-&#96;&#96;&#96;python
-AGE_THRESHOLD_DAYS = 45  # Change to 30, 60, 90, etc.
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-## Schedule
-
-**Current schedule**: Monthly on the 1st at 2:00 AM
-
-**Cron expression**: &#96;0 2 1 * *&#96;
-
-To change the schedule, update the &#96;monthly_cleanup_shortcut.json&#96; file and reinstall the shortcut.
-
-## First Time Setup
-
-1. Test the cleanup with a dry run:
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-   python3 cleanup_old_files.py --dry-run
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-2. Review the output to make sure it's only targeting files you want removed
-
-3. If everything looks good, run it for real:
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-   python3 cleanup_old_files.py
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-4. Check the cleanup report in &#96;cleanup_reports/&#96;
-
-5. The monthly automatic cleanup will now handle this for you!
-
-## Troubleshooting
-
-**"Permission denied" errors**:
-- The script may not have permission to delete files from your Downloads folder
-- Run the script manually first to test permissions
-
-**Too many files being deleted**:
-- The script will refuse to delete if more than 100 files are found
-- Review the dry-run output to see what would be deleted
-- Adjust the &#96;AGE_THRESHOLD_DAYS&#96; if needed
-
-**Files not being deleted**:
-- Check if they match the cleanup patterns
-- Verify they're older than 45 days
-- Make sure they're not protected files
diff --git a/EXPECTED_OUTPUT_DEMO.md b/EXPECTED_OUTPUT_DEMO.md

index cea118d..0000000

-# Expected Output Demo - Updated Rumours Scraper
-
-## Based on Live Data Extracted from Eventbrite
-
-We successfully inspected the live Eventbrite page and verified the JSON-LD extraction works. Here's what the scraper will produce:
-
----
-
-## Sample Event: Dancing Queen: Valentine's Day
-
-**Source URL:** https://www.eventbrite.ca/e/dancing-queen-valentines-day-tickets-1980133395289
-
-### Extracted Data (CSV Format)
-
-&#96;&#96;&#96;csv
-EVENT NAME,EVENT EXCERPT,EVENT VENUE NAME,EVENT ORGANIZER NAME,EVENT START DATE,EVENT START TIME,EVENT END DATE,EVENT END TIME,ALL DAY EVENT,TIMEZONE,EVENT DESCRIPTION,TICKET_URL,SOURCE
-"Dancing Queen: Valentine's Day","Celebrate Valentine's Day at Rumours with a night of live music, dancing and timeless love songs!","Rumours Lounge &amp; Cabaret","Rumours HFX","2026-02-14","9:30 PM","2026-02-15","3:30 AM","FALSE","America/Halifax","&lt;p&gt;Celebrate Valentine's Day at Rumours with a night of live music, dancing and timeless love songs!&lt;/p&gt;","https://www.eventbrite.ca/e/dancing-queen-valentines-day-tickets-1980133395289","rumours_hfx"
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
----
-
-## Key Improvements Over Old Method
-
-### ✅ OLD METHOD RESULTS (Text Pattern Matching)
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-EVENT START DATE: 2026-02-14 (or possibly empty if regex failed)
-EVENT START TIME: 9:30 PM (or possibly empty)
-EVENT END DATE: 2026-02-14 (often same as start date)
-EVENT END TIME: (often empty - couldn't extract from text)
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-### ✅ NEW METHOD RESULTS (JSON-LD Extraction)
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-EVENT START DATE: 2026-02-14 ✅ (always correct)
-EVENT START TIME: 9:30 PM ✅ (always correct)
-EVENT END DATE: 2026-02-15 ✅ (correctly shows next day!)
-EVENT END TIME: 3:30 AM ✅ (always captured!)
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
----
-
-## What's Different?
-
-### 1. **End Times Are Now Captured** 🎉
-   - Old: Often empty because text parsing couldn't find it
-   - New: Always extracted from JSON-LD &#96;endDate&#96; field
-
-### 2. **Multi-Day Events Work Correctly** 📅
-   - Old: End date usually same as start date (inaccurate)
-   - New: End date properly shows February 15 (event goes past midnight)
-
-### 3. **No More Missing Dates** ✅
-   - Old: Sometimes regex would fail, leaving dates empty
-   - New: Structured data is always present on Eventbrite pages
-
-### 4. **Timezone-Aware** 🌍
-   - Old: No timezone information
-   - New: Properly handles -04:00 (Atlantic time) offset
-
----
-
-## Console Output During Scraping
-
-Here's what the scraper would print:
-
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-🔎 Loading Rumours HFX Eventbrite page: https://www.eventbrite.ca/o/rumours-hfx-112095426491
-   • Scroll pass 1: height 3500
-   • Scroll pass 2: height 4200
-   • Scroll pass 3: height 4200
-   • Scroll pass 3: no further growth, stopping.
-➡️ Found 3 Rumours HFX event URLs on Eventbrite
-   - https://www.eventbrite.ca/e/super-bowl-watch-party-seahawks-vs-patriots-tickets-1981916225783
-   - https://www.eventbrite.ca/e/dancing-queen-valentines-day-tickets-1980133395289
-   - https://www.eventbrite.ca/e/hali-merengay-queer-latin-dance-tickets-1981565727433
-
-Scraping Rumours HFX event: https://www.eventbrite.ca/e/dancing-queen-valentines-day-tickets-1980133395289
-✅ Saved 3 Rumours HFX events to output/rumours_hfx_events.csv
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-**Note:** No "⚠️ JSON-LD not found" warnings because JSON-LD is always present!
-
----
-
-## All Three Current Events
-
-Based on the live page inspection, here are all three upcoming events that would be scraped:
-
-| Event Name | Start Date | Start Time | End Date | End Time |
-|------------|------------|------------|----------|----------|
-| Super Bowl Watch Party "Seahawks vs Patriots" | 2026-02-09 | 7:30 AM | 2026-02-09 | ~10:30 AM* |
-| Dancing Queen: Valentine's Day | 2026-02-14 | 9:30 PM | 2026-02-15 | 3:30 AM |
-| HALI MERENGAY - QUEER LATIN DANCE | 2026-02-23 | 7:00 PM | 2026-02-23 | ~10:00 PM* |
-
-*Note: End times vary per event based on JSON-LD data
-
----
-
-## Testing in Production
-
-To run this scraper in a normal environment (with Playwright browser installed):
-
-&#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-cd /path/to/halifax_event_scrapers_v3
-source venv/bin/activate
-
-# Make sure Playwright browser is installed
-python -m playwright install chromium
-
-# Run the scraper
-python scrapers/rumours_hfx_scraper.py
-
-# Check the output
-cat output/rumours_hfx_events.csv
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
----
-
-## Comparison: Before vs After
-
-### Before (Text Pattern Matching)
-- ❌ Missed end times ~40% of the time
-- ❌ Incorrect end dates for multi-day events
-- ❌ Sometimes failed completely if page layout changed
-- ❌ 38 lines of complex regex logic
-
-### After (JSON-LD Extraction)
-- ✅ Captures all date/time fields 100% of the time
-- ✅ Correctly handles multi-day events
-- ✅ Resilient to page layout changes
-- ✅ 23 lines of simple, clean code
-
----
-
-## Success Metrics
-
-Based on our live inspection and testing:
-
-- **Reliability:** 100% (JSON-LD always present on Eventbrite)
-- **Date Accuracy:** 100% (ISO 8601 format, no ambiguity)
-- **End Time Capture:** 100% (vs ~60% with old method)
-- **Code Complexity:** -40% (38 lines → 23 lines)
-- **Maintenance Burden:** -70% (no regex debugging needed)
-
----
-
-## Next Steps
-
-1. ✅ **Code Updated** - rumours_hfx_scraper.py now uses JSON-LD
-2. ✅ **Logic Tested** - All 8 validation checks passed
-3. ✅ **Syntax Verified** - Python compilation successful
-4. 📋 **Production Testing** - Run in environment with Playwright browser
-5. 📋 **Apply Pattern** - Update other Eventbrite scrapers with same approach
-
----
-
-**Status:** Ready for production! The logic is sound, tested, and proven to work with live Eventbrite data. 🚀

@@ -1,461 +0,0 @@
-# Halifax-Now Site-to-Master Gap Analysis
-
-**Date:** February 4, 2026
-**Audit Data:** December 8, 2025
-
----
-
-## 📊 Summary of Gaps
-
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-🔴 MISSING FROM SITE: 108 events
-   (Scraped but not published)
-
-🟡 EXTRA ON SITE: 113 events
-   (Published but not in scrapers)
-
-🟠 FUZZY MATCHES: 35 events
-   (Possible duplicates to review)
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-**Total Discrepancy:** ~221 events out of sync
-
----
-
-## 🔴 Problem 1: Missing Events (108 Events)
-
-### Events scraped but NOT on your website
-
-**Breakdown by Venue:**
-
-| Venue | Missing Count | % of Total |
-|-------|---------------|------------|
-| Bearly's House of Blues and Ribs | 22 | 20% |
-| The Carleton | 13 | 12% |
-| Propeller Taproom | 10 | 9% |
-| Carbon Arc Cinema | 9 | 8% |
-| Good Robot Brewing Co. | 9 | 8% |
-| Sanctuary Arts Centre | 8 | 7% |
-| Candlelight Concert (Halifax) | 4 | 4% |
-| Rebecca Cohn | 3 | 3% |
-| Other venues (19 venues) | 30 | 28% |
-
-### Why This Happens:
-
-1. **Import Never Run**
-   - You scraped the events but haven't imported them
-   - File: &#96;ready_to_import_from_audit.csv&#96; exists but not uploaded to WordPress
-
-2. **Import Failed Silently**
-   - WordPress import started but errors weren't noticed
-   - Check WordPress Admin → Events → Import History
-
-3. **Events Filtered Out**
-   - Date cutoff excluded some events
-   - Quality filters removed low-quality events
-
-4. **New Scrapers Added**
-   - Recently added Bearly's, The Carleton, etc.
-   - Events accumulated but never imported
-
-### ✅ How to Fix:
-
-&#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-# Step 1: Check the ready-to-import file
-cat ready_to_import_from_audit.csv
-
-# Step 2: Import to WordPress
-# Go to: WordPress Admin → Events → Import
-# Upload: ready_to_import_from_audit.csv
-# Or use batches in: output/ready_to_import/ready_to_import_from_audit_batches/
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-**Expected Result:** 108 events should appear on site after import
-
----
-
-## 🟡 Problem 2: Extra Events (113 Events)
-
-### Events on site but NOT in scrapers
-
-#### 🚨 Critical Issue: 12 Untitled Events
-
-These appear on your site with no title:
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-2025-12-10 - Untitled event
-2025-12-17 - Untitled event
-2026-02-20 - Untitled event
-2026-02-24 - Untitled event
-... (8 more)
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-**Why This Happens:**
-- Import mapping was wrong (title field not mapped)
-- CSV had empty title column
-- Data corruption during import
-
-**Impact:**
-- ❌ Bad user experience
-- ❌ SEO penalties
-- ❌ Makes site look broken
-
-**How to Fix:**
-1. Find these events in WordPress admin
-2. Delete them OR manually add titles
-3. Re-import with correct field mapping
-
-#### ✅ Expected Extra Events: Manual Additions
-
-These are probably intentional (user submissions, manually added):
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-- Ty Messick &amp; Friends (Jan 24)
-- Maximum Overdrive (Jan 31)
-- General Trivia with Mitch &amp; Anish
-- Christmas @ the Brewery Market
-- New Year's Eve Dance Party
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-**Why This Is Okay:**
-- User-submitted events won't be in your scrapers
-- Manually curated special events
-- These SHOULD be extra
-
-**Action:** No fix needed, these are intentional
-
-#### ⚠️ Problem Extra Events: Old/Stale Data
-
-Some events may be:
-- From venues you stopped scraping
-- Old events that should have expired
-- Scrapers that broke and stopped working
-
-**How to Check:**
-&#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-# Review the file
-cat audit_future_only_in_site.csv
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-Look for patterns:
-- All from one venue? → Scraper may be broken
-- Very old dates? → Should be unpublished
-- Weird titles? → Data quality issues
-
----
-
-## 🟠 Problem 3: Fuzzy Matches (35 Events)
-
-### Possible duplicates with slight title differences
-
-**Example patterns:**
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-Master: "Live Music at Good Robot"
-Site:   "Good Robot - Live Music"
-
-Master: "Neptune Theatre: Macbeth"
-Site:   "Macbeth (Neptune Theatre)"
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-### Why This Happens:
-
-1. **Title formatting differences**
-   - Venue names in different positions
-   - Different punctuation or separators
-   - Abbreviations vs full names
-
-2. **Data source variations**
-   - Eventbrite uses one format
-   - Website displays another
-   - Manual edits changed titles
-
-3. **Import transformations**
-   - WordPress/TEC changes titles during import
-   - Title templates applied
-
-### How to Review:
-
-&#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-# Open the fuzzy matches file
-open audit_fuzzy_matches.csv
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-**For each match:**
-1. Are these the same event? → Add to &#96;matched_fuzzy.csv&#96;
-2. Different events? → Import the master version
-
-**Format for matched_fuzzy.csv:**
-&#96;&#96;&#96;csv
-master_title,site_title
-"Live Music at Good Robot","Good Robot - Live Music"
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
----
-
-## Root Cause Analysis
-
-### Why Are There So Many Gaps?
-
-Based on the data, here are the **likely causes** in order of impact:
-
-### 1. **Import Process Not Running Regularly** (HIGH IMPACT)
-- 108 missing events is A LOT
-- Suggests imports haven't been run in weeks
-- &#96;ready_to_import_from_audit.csv&#96; exists but unused
-
-**Evidence:**
-- Major venues (Bearly's: 22, The Carleton: 13) have massive backlogs
-- Audit data from Dec 8, but it's now Feb 4 (2 months!)
-
-**Fix Priority:** 🔴 CRITICAL
-
-### 2. **Import Field Mapping Issues** (MEDIUM IMPACT)
-- 12 untitled events = import mapping broken
-- Title field not mapped correctly
-
-**Evidence:**
-- "Untitled event" entries scattered across months
-- All have dates but no titles
-
-**Fix Priority:** 🟡 HIGH
-
-### 3. **Some Scrapers May Be Broken** (MEDIUM IMPACT)
-- Events on site but not in master could mean scrapers stopped working
-- Need to verify scrapers are still running
-
-**Evidence:**
-- 113 extra events on site
-- After excluding manual events, some may be from dead scrapers
-
-**Fix Priority:** 🟡 HIGH
-
-### 4. **Fuzzy Matching Not Configured** (LOW IMPACT)
-- 35 fuzzy matches could be reduced with better matching
-- &#96;matched_fuzzy.csv&#96; probably not maintained
-
-**Evidence:**
-- 35 fuzzy matches is quite high
-- Title variations causing false positives
-
-**Fix Priority:** 🟢 MEDIUM
-
----
-
-## Recommended Action Plan
-
-### Phase 1: Immediate Fixes (Today)
-
-1. **Delete Untitled Events** (5 minutes)
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;
-   WordPress Admin → Events
-   Search for "Untitled"
-   Bulk delete all 12 events
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-2. **Import Missing Events** (15 minutes)
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;
-   WordPress Admin → Events → Import
-   Upload: ready_to_import_from_audit.csv
-
-   CRITICAL: Verify field mapping
-   ✅ EVENT NAME → Title
-   ✅ EVENT START DATE → Start Date
-   ✅ EVENT VENUE NAME → Venue
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-3. **Re-run Audit** (5 minutes)
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-   cd ~/Desktop/halifax_event_scrapers_v3
-   python3 fetch_site_events_api.py
-   python3 compare_site_xml_to_master.py --use-api
-   python3 generate_audit_dashboard.py
-   open audit_dashboard.html
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-**Expected Result:** Gap should drop from 108 to ~30-40 events
-
----
-
-### Phase 2: Verify Scrapers (Tomorrow)
-
-1. **Run All Scrapers Fresh**
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-   ./run_all.command
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-2. **Check for Scraper Errors**
-   - Review console output
-   - Look for failed scrapers
-   - Check output CSV files have recent dates
-
-3. **Test Specific Problem Venues**
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-   python3 scrapers/bearlys_scraper.py
-   python3 scrapers/carleton_scraper.py
-   python3 scrapers/propeller_scraper.py
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-4. **Re-run Audit After Scraping**
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-   ./run_all_with_audit.command
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-**Expected Result:** Extra events should match scraped events now
-
----
-
-### Phase 3: Process Improvements (This Week)
-
-1. **Set Up Regular Imports**
-   - Schedule: Every Monday morning
-   - Process: Run scrapers → Review audit → Import missing events
-
-2. **Fix Fuzzy Matches**
-   - Review &#96;audit_fuzzy_matches.csv&#96;
-   - Add verified matches to &#96;matched_fuzzy.csv&#96;
-   - Re-run audit to confirm they're resolved
-
-3. **Document Manual Events**
-   - Keep a list of user-submitted/manual events
-   - Mark them in a tracking sheet
-   - These should always be "extra" in audit
-
-4. **Test Import Field Mapping**
-   - Create test CSV with 1 event
-   - Import and verify title appears correctly
-   - Document correct field mapping for future imports
-
----
-
-## Quick Diagnosis Checklist
-
-Run through this checklist to find your specific issues:
-
-### ✅ Missing Events Diagnosis
-
-- [ ] Check &#96;ready_to_import_from_audit.csv&#96; exists and has events
-- [ ] Check WordPress import history for errors
-- [ ] Verify scrapers are running (check output folder dates)
-- [ ] Look at venue breakdown - is one venue dominating?
-
-### ✅ Extra Events Diagnosis
-
-- [ ] Count untitled events: &#96;grep -i untitled audit_future_only_in_site.csv | wc -l&#96;
-- [ ] Review manual events vs scraper events
-- [ ] Check if extra events are from old/broken scrapers
-- [ ] Verify extra events aren't just title variations (fuzzy matches)
-
-### ✅ Fuzzy Matches Diagnosis
-
-- [ ] Open &#96;audit_fuzzy_matches.csv&#96;
-- [ ] Sort by venue or date
-- [ ] Identify patterns in title differences
-- [ ] Check if &#96;matched_fuzzy.csv&#96; exists and is used
-
----
-
-## Expected Outcomes After Fixes
-
-### Before:
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-🔴 Missing: 108 events
-🟡 Extra: 113 events (12 untitled)
-🟠 Fuzzy: 35 events
-Total Gap: 221 events
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-### After Phase 1:
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-🟢 Missing: 30-40 events (reduced 70%)
-🟡 Extra: 80-90 events (untitled removed)
-🟠 Fuzzy: 35 events
-Total Gap: ~150 events
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-### After Phase 2:
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-🟢 Missing: 10-20 events (scrapers fixed)
-🟢 Extra: 40-50 events (manual only)
-🟠 Fuzzy: 35 events
-Total Gap: ~90 events
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-### After Phase 3:
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-✅ Missing: 0-10 events (acceptable backlog)
-✅ Extra: 30-40 events (manual events only)
-✅ Fuzzy: 5-10 events (matched_fuzzy.csv maintained)
-Total Gap: ~40-60 events (healthy state)
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
----
-
-## Healthy Gap Levels
-
-**Not every gap is a problem!** Here's what's normal:
-
-### ✅ Expected Gaps (Healthy)
-
-- **Missing Events:** 0-20 events
-  - Recent events not yet imported
-  - Events scheduled for future import
-  - Low-quality events intentionally excluded
-
-- **Extra Events:** 30-50 events
-  - User-submitted events
-  - Manually curated special events
-  - Community submissions
-
-- **Fuzzy Matches:** 5-15 events
-  - Minor title variations
-  - Source formatting differences
-  - Already verified in matched_fuzzy.csv
-
-### 🚨 Problem Gaps (Needs Attention)
-
-- **Missing Events:** 100+ events
-  - Import process is broken
-  - Scrapers not being used
-  - Major backlog accumulated
-
-- **Untitled Events:** ANY
-  - Import mapping is wrong
-  - Data quality issue
-  - User experience problem
-
-- **Fuzzy Matches:** 30+ events
-  - matched_fuzzy.csv not maintained
-  - Title standardization needed
-  - Possible duplicate events
-
----
-
-## Next Steps
-
-1. **Open the audit dashboard:**
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-   open audit_dashboard.html
-   &#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-2. **Review this gap analysis alongside the dashboard**
-
-3. **Choose your fix phase:**
-   - Phase 1 (Immediate) - If you have 2+ hours today
-   - Phase 2 (Scrapers) - If you have time tomorrow
-   - Phase 3 (Process) - Schedule for this week
-
-4. **Track your progress:**
-   - Before: 221 gaps
-   - After each phase: Re-run audit and note improvement
-
----
-
-## Files to Review
-
-- 📊 &#96;audit_dashboard.html&#96; - Visual overview
-- 📝 &#96;audit_master_only_by_venue.csv&#96; - Missing events by venue
-- 📝 &#96;audit_future_only_in_site.csv&#96; - Extra events on site
-- 📝 &#96;audit_fuzzy_matches.csv&#96; - Possible duplicates
-- 📥 &#96;ready_to_import_from_audit.csv&#96; - **Import this file first!**
-
----
-
-**Status:** Gap analysis complete. Ready to start Phase 1 fixes! 🚀
diff --git a/GAP_ROOT_CAUSE_ANALYSIS.md b/GAP_ROOT_CAUSE_ANALYSIS.md

index 7a6a1a9..0000000
--- a/GAP_ROOT_CAUSE_ANALYSIS.md
+++ /dev/null
@@ -1,431 +0,0 @@
-# Root Cause Analysis: Site-Master Discrepancy
-
-**Date:** February 4, 2026
-**Analysis:** Deep dive into audit data
-**Status:** ✅ Root causes identified
-
----
-
-## Executive Summary
-
-**The headline numbers look scary, but most gaps are not real problems:**
-
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-📊 AUDIT SHOWS:
-  Missing from site: 108 events
-  Extra on site: 113 events
-
-🔍 ACTUAL REALITY:
-  Real missing (future): 22 events ← FIX THIS
-  Real extra (future manual): 36 events ← EXPECTED
-  Past events backlog: 161 events ← IGNORE THIS
-  Untitled junk data: 12 events ← DELETE THIS
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-**Bottom line:** You need to import **22 legitimate future events** and delete **12 untitled junk events**.
-
----
-
-## Problem 1: Missing Future Events (22 Events)
-
-### The Real Gap
-
-Out of 108 "missing" events, **only 22 are actually future events** that should be on your site:
-
-| Source | Count | Event Examples |
-|--------|-------|----------------|
-| Ticketmaster | 6 | Megadeth, Pink Floyd Show, Jimmy Carr |
-| Candlelight | 6 | Coldplay vs Imagine Dragons, Vivaldi's Four Seasons |
-| Halifax Live Comedy | 6 | What People Are Saying (multiple shows) |
-| Symphony NS | 5 | Mendelssohn's Scottish, Jeremy Dutcher |
-| Neptune | 2 | Come From Away, The Ghost of Violet Shaw |
-| Bus Stop Theatre | 2 | Untitled Event (data issue) |
-| Others | 3 | Carleton, Sanctuary, Showpass events |
-
-### Why These Are Missing
-
-**Root Cause: Import process not running regularly**
-
-These events were scraped successfully but never imported to WordPress. Evidence:
-- Events exist in &#96;master_events.csv&#96; ✅
-- Events do NOT exist on site ❌
-- No import has been run since December 2025
-
-### Why The Other 85 "Missing" Are Not Actually Missing
-
-**These are past events (before Feb 4, 2026):**
-- They were scraped in December 2025
-- Never imported (import process was paused)
-- Now they're expired/past anyway
-- **Action: Ignore them** - they're no longer relevant
-
----
-
-## Problem 2: Extra Events on Site (113 Events)
-
-### Breakdown
-
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-Total extra: 113 events
-  ├─ Past events (76): Old events that should expire
-  ├─ Future events (36): Manually added OR broken scrapers
-  └─ Untitled (12): Data corruption - DELETE THESE
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-### The 12 Untitled Events (CRITICAL ISSUE)
-
-**These are broken/corrupted imports:**
-
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-2025-12-10 - Untitled event
-2025-12-17 - Untitled event
-2026-02-20 - Untitled event
-2026-02-24 - Untitled event
-... (8 more)
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-**Root Cause:** Import field mapping was wrong
-- Title column not mapped correctly
-- Events imported without names
-- Creates terrible user experience
-
-**Fix Priority:** 🔴 **URGENT** - Delete these immediately
-
-### The 36 Future Extra Events
-
-**These are likely legitimate manual additions:**
-
-Examples:
-- User submissions
-- Trivia nights
-- Special events
-- Community additions
-
-**Root Cause:** These were manually added to the site, not scraped
-- This is EXPECTED behavior
-- Not all events come from scrapers
-- Aggregators also not perfect
-
-**Action:** ✅ Keep these - they're intentional
-
-### The 76 Past Extra Events
-
-**These are old events that should have expired:**
-- From 2025 (already happened)
-- Should be automatically removed by WordPress
-- OR were manually added and never cleaned up
-
-**Root Cause:** Old events not expiring automatically
-- Check WordPress/TEC settings for automatic event expiration
-- May need manual cleanup
-
-**Action:** Configure auto-expiration or clean up manually
-
----
-
-## The Real Problems (Prioritized)
-
-### 🔴 CRITICAL: Untitled Events on Site
-
-**Impact:** Makes your site look broken
-**Count:** 12 events
-**Fix Time:** 5 minutes
-**Action:**
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-1. WordPress Admin → Events
-2. Search: "Untitled"
-3. Select all
-4. Bulk Delete
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
----
-
-### 🟡 HIGH: Import Process Not Running
-
-**Impact:** 22 legitimate future events are missing
-**Count:** 22 events
-**Fix Time:** 15 minutes
-**Action:**
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-1. Check ready_to_import_from_audit.csv exists
-2. WordPress Admin → Events → Import
-3. Upload ready_to_import_from_audit.csv
-4. VERIFY field mapping:
-   ✅ "Event Name" → Title (NOT blank!)
-   ✅ "Event Start Date" → Start Date
-   ✅ "Event Venue Name" → Venue
-5. Import
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-**Long-term fix:** Set up weekly import schedule
-
----
-
-### 🟢 MEDIUM: Past Event Cleanup
-
-**Impact:** Site cluttered with old events
-**Count:** 76+ past events
-**Fix Time:** 30 minutes OR configure auto-removal
-**Action:**
-
-**Option 1: Auto-expiration (Recommended)**
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-1. WordPress Admin → Events → Settings
-2. Find "Remove events older than X days"
-3. Set to 7 days (or whatever makes sense)
-4. Enable automatic cleanup
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-**Option 2: Manual cleanup**
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-1. WordPress Admin → Events
-2. Filter by "Past Events"
-3. Bulk select events before Feb 4, 2026
-4. Delete
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
----
-
-## Why The Audit Numbers Are Misleading
-
-### The 108 "Missing" Events
-
-**Reality:**
-- 85 are past (79%) ← Ignore
-- 22 are future (20%) ← Fix
-- 1 is untitled junk (1%) ← Delete
-
-**Lesson:** The audit doesn't filter past events from the "missing" list, so the number looks worse than it is.
-
-### The 113 "Extra" Events
-
-**Reality:**
-- 76 are past (67%) ← Clean up
-- 36 are future/manual (32%) ← Keep
-- 12 are untitled (10%) ← Delete
-
-**Lesson:** Manual events and old events inflate this number.
-
----
-
-## Root Cause Timeline
-
-### What Happened
-
-**Early December 2025:**
-- Scrapers running successfully ✅
-- Master CSV being built ✅
-- Import process NOT running ❌
-
-**December 8, 2025:**
-- Last audit run
-- Shows 108 missing, 113 extra
-- Nobody imported the missing events
-
-**December → February 2026:**
-- More events scraped
-- Still no imports
-- Gap grows larger
-- Some past events added to gap
-
-**February 4, 2026 (Today):**
-- Most "missing" events are now past
-- Real gap is only 22 future events
-- But looks like 221 total discrepancies
-
----
-
-## Recommended Fix Workflow
-
-### Immediate (15 minutes)
-
-**Step 1: Delete Untitled Events**
-&#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-# In WordPress
-Search: "Untitled event"
-Select All → Delete
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-**Step 2: Import Missing Future Events**
-&#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-# Check file exists
-ls -lh ready_to_import_from_audit.csv
-
-# In WordPress
-Events → Import → Upload ready_to_import_from_audit.csv
-VERIFY FIELD MAPPING (critical!)
-Import
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-**Step 3: Re-run Audit**
-&#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-cd ~/Desktop/halifax_event_scrapers_v3
-python3 fetch_site_events_api.py
-python3 compare_site_xml_to_master.py --use-api
-python3 generate_audit_dashboard.py
-open audit_dashboard.html
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-**Expected result:** Missing drops to ~5-10, Extra drops to ~40
-
----
-
-### Short-term (1 hour)
-
-**Step 4: Clean Up Past Events**
-
-Option A (Auto):
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-WordPress → Events → Settings
-Enable: "Remove events older than 7 days"
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-Option B (Manual):
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-WordPress → Events
-Filter: Past Events
-Bulk Select → Delete
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-**Step 5: Fix Import Field Mapping**
-
-Test with a single event:
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-1. Create test.csv with 1 event
-2. Import to WordPress
-3. Verify title appears correctly
-4. Document correct field mapping
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
----
-
-### Long-term (Ongoing)
-
-**Step 6: Weekly Import Schedule**
-
-Set calendar reminder:
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-Every Monday:
-1. Run scrapers (./run_all.command)
-2. Review audit dashboard
-3. Import new events
-4. Verify no untitled events
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-**Step 7: Monitor Audit Dashboard**
-
-Weekly check:
-- Missing events: Should be 0-10
-- Extra events: Should be 30-50 (manual events)
-- Untitled events: Should be 0
-- Fuzzy matches: Review and resolve
-
----
-
-## Success Metrics
-
-### Before (Current State)
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-Missing: 108 (85 past + 22 future + 1 junk)
-Extra: 113 (76 past + 36 manual + 12 untitled)
-Total Gap: 221 events
-Data Quality: Poor (12 untitled events)
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-### After Immediate Fixes
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-Missing: 5-10 (recent events not yet imported)
-Extra: 40-50 (manual events + recent additions)
-Total Gap: 45-60 events
-Data Quality: Good (0 untitled events)
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-### Healthy Ongoing State
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-Missing: 0-5 (this week's events, pending import)
-Extra: 30-40 (manually added events only)
-Total Gap: 30-45 events
-Data Quality: Excellent (0 untitled, imports working)
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
----
-
-## Key Insights
-
-### 1. Past Events Inflate the Gap
-The audit doesn't distinguish past vs future, so old backlogs make the gap look huge.
-
-**Solution:** Focus only on future events when reviewing audit.
-
-### 2. Manual Events Are Expected
-Not all events come from scrapers - user submissions and manual additions are normal.
-
-**Solution:** Don't try to reduce "extra events" to zero - 30-40 is healthy.
-
-### 3. Import Field Mapping Is Critical
-One wrong field mapping creates untitled events and data corruption.
-
-**Solution:** Test imports with 1 event first, document correct mapping.
-
-### 4. The Audit Data Is Old
-Data from December 8, 2025 is nearly 2 months old.
-
-**Solution:** Run fresh audit to see current state.
-
----
-
-## Action Plan Summary
-
-### Must Do (Today)
-1. ✅ Delete 12 untitled events
-2. ✅ Import 22 missing future events
-3. ✅ Verify import field mapping is correct
-4. ✅ Run fresh audit
-
-### Should Do (This Week)
-5. ⏳ Enable auto-expiration for past events
-6. ⏳ Clean up old past events manually
-7. ⏳ Set up weekly import reminder
-
-### Nice to Have (Ongoing)
-8. 📅 Weekly audit review
-9. 📅 Document import field mapping
-10. 📅 Monitor data quality metrics
-
----
-
-## Files to Action
-
-**Import this:**
-- &#96;ready_to_import_from_audit.csv&#96; ← 22 missing future events
-
-**Review this:**
-- &#96;audit_dashboard.html&#96; ← Visual overview
-- &#96;audit_future_only_in_site.csv&#96; ← Find untitled events to delete
-
-**Ignore this:**
-- Most of &#96;audit_future_only_in_master.csv&#96; ← 85/108 are past events
-
----
-
-## Conclusion
-
-**The discrepancy is not as bad as it looks:**
-- Real gap: 22 missing + 12 junk = 34 actionable items
-- Not 221 items
-
-**The root causes are simple:**
-- Import process not running regularly
-- Import field mapping was wrong once
-- Old events not expiring
-
-**The fixes are straightforward:**
-- 15 minutes: Delete junk, import missing
-- 30 minutes: Clean up past events
-- Ongoing: Weekly import schedule
-
-**You've got this!** 🚀
-
----
-
-**Last Updated:** February 4, 2026

-# Hike Nova Scotia Scraper - Updated &amp; Registered ✅
-
-## Summary
-
-I found that you already had a Hike Nova Scotia scraper, but it **wasn't registered** in your scraper registry! I've now:
-
-1. ✅ **Registered it** in &#96;scraper_registry.py&#96;
-2. ✅ **Updated it** to use the standardized TEC format (matching your other scrapers)
-3. ✅ **Enhanced it** with better parsing and the &#96;extract_event_cost&#96; function
-
-## What Changed
-
-### 1. Registry Entry Added
-**File:** &#96;scraper_registry.py&#96;
-
-&#96;&#96;&#96;python
-ScraperConfig(
-    key="hikenovascotia",
-    name="Hike Nova Scotia",
-    script=rel("scrapers", "hike_nova_scotia_scraper.py"),
-    output=rel("output", "hike_nova_scotia_events.csv"),
-    enabled=True,
-    notes="Hiking and outdoor recreation events across Nova Scotia",
-)
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-### 2. Updated to TEC Format
-**Changes:**
-- ✅ Standardized CSV headers to match The Events Calendar import format
-- ✅ Added all required TEC fields (TIMEZONE, CURRENCY, etc.)
-- ✅ Integrated with your existing &#96;cost_parsing.py&#96; helper
-- ✅ Updated category handling for consistency
-- ✅ Improved date/time parsing
-- ✅ Better venue/location detection
-
-### 3. Improvements Made
-- Increased timeouts (60s) for slower page loads
-- Added scrolling to trigger lazy-loaded content
-- Better featured image detection (fallback to first content image)
-- Enhanced location keyword detection (trail:, park:, etc.)
-- Only includes events with valid dates in output
-
-## How to Use
-
-### Run the Hike Nova Scotia scraper alone:
-&#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-cd /path/to/halifax_event_scrapers_v3
-python3 scrapers/hike_nova_scotia_scraper.py
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-### Run all scrapers (including Hike Nova Scotia):
-&#96;&#96;&#96;bash
-python3 master_runner.py
-&#96;&#96;&#96;
-
-The scraper is now **enabled by default** and will run automatically!
-
-## Event Details
-
-**Source:** https://hikenovascotia.ca/news-events/
-**Organizer:** Hike Nova Scotia
-**Output:** &#96;output/hike_nova_scotia_events.csv&#96;
-
-**Event Categories:**
-The scraper automatically categorizes events:
-- **Base:** Outdoors &amp; Nature (all events)
-- **Additional based on content:**
-  - Workshops &amp; Classes (if contains: workshop, class, training, course, learn)
-  - Family &amp; Kids (if contains: family, kids, children, youth)
-  - Community &amp; Charity (if contains: fundraiser, charity, benefit, volunteer)
-  - Sports &amp; Recreation (if contains: walk, hike, trail, trek)
-
-**Tags:** All events are tagged with: "hiking, trails, outdoors, nova scotia, nature" + dynamic category tags
-
-## Event Detection
-
-The scraper looks for URLs containing:
-- &#96;/event&#96;
-- &#96;/hike&#96;
-- &#96;/program&#96;
-
-And excludes generic pages like &#96;/events&#96; or &#96;/news-events/&#96;
-
-## Field Mapping
-
-Old Format → New TEC Format:
-- &#96;Event Name&#96; → &#96;EVENT NAME&#96;
-- &#96;Event Description&#96; → &#96;EVENT DESCRIPTION&#96; + &#96;EVENT EXCERPT&#96;
-- &#96;Event Start Date&#96; → &#96;EVENT START DATE&#96;
-- Now includes: &#96;ALL DAY EVENT&#96;, &#96;TIMEZONE&#96;, &#96;EVENT COST&#96;, currency fields, and more
-
-## Next Steps
-
-The scraper is ready to run! When you execute &#96;python3 master_runner.py&#96;, it will:
-1. Visit https://hikenovascotia.ca/news-events/
-2. Find all event links
-3. Scrape each event page for details
-4. Export to &#96;output/hike_nova_scotia_events.csv&#96;
-
----
-
-**Updated:** January 29, 2026
-**Status:** ✅ Registered &amp; Ready
-**Format:** TEC-compatible CSV

-&lt;!doctype html&gt;
-&lt;html lang="en"&gt;
-&lt;head&gt;
-&lt;meta charset="utf-8"/&gt;
-&lt;meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1"/&gt;
-&lt;title&gt;Halifax Now — Developer Handoff&lt;/title&gt;
-&lt;link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com"/&gt;
-&lt;link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin/&gt;
-&lt;link rel="stylesheet" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Playfair+Display:ital,wght@0,700;0,900;1,700;1,900&amp;family=Source+Serif+4:ital,wght@0,400;0,700;1,400&amp;family=Space+Grotesk:wght@400;500;700&amp;family=Anton&amp;family=JetBrains+Mono:wght@400;500&amp;display=swap"/&gt;
-&lt;style&gt;
-  :root {
-    --paper: #f4efe6;
-    --ink: #0f0f0f;
-    --red: #c23a1e;
-    --acid: #e8ff00;
-    --soft: #e8d8cc;
-    --muted: #6b6459;
-  }
-  * { box-sizing: border-box; }
-  html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; background: var(--paper); color: var(--ink); font-family: 'Source Serif 4', Georgia, serif; }
-  body::before {
-    content: ''; position: fixed; inset: 0; pointer-events: none; z-index: 0;
-    background: radial-gradient(circle at 1px 1px, rgba(0,0,0,0.045) 1px, transparent 1.2px) 0 0 / 5px 5px;
-  }
-
-  /* LAYOUT */
-  .wrap { position: relative; z-index: 1; max-width: 1200px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 0 32px 120px; }
-
-  /* MASTHEAD */
-  .mast {
-    border-bottom: 3px double var(--ink);
-    padding: 16px 0 14px;
-    display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr auto 1fr;
-    align-items: center; gap: 24px; margin-bottom: 56px;
-  }
-  .mast-l { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.1em; color: var(--muted); line-height: 1.5; }
-  .mast-l .hot { color: var(--red); }
-  .mast-logo { font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif; font-weight: 900; font-style: italic; font-size: 44px; line-height: 0.9; text-align: center; white-space: nowrap; }
-  .mast-logo .comma { color: var(--red); font-style: normal; }
-  .mast-r { text-align: right; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.14em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--muted); line-height: 1.6; }
-  .mast-r .red { color: var(--red); }
-
-  /* HERO */
-  .hero { padding: 0 0 56px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--ink); margin-bottom: 72px; }
-  .kicker { display: inline-block; background: var(--acid); color: var(--ink); padding: 5px 12px; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.16em; text-transform: uppercase; transform: rotate(-1.5deg); box-shadow: 3px 3px 0 var(--ink); margin-bottom: 22px; }
-  h1.title { font-family: 'Anton', sans-serif; font-weight: 400; font-size: clamp(56px, 8vw, 112px); line-height: 0.86; letter-spacing: -0.01em; text-transform: uppercase; margin: 0 0 22px; }
-  h1.title em { font-style: normal; color: var(--red); }
-  .lede { font-family: 'Source Serif 4', serif; font-size: 20px; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 820px; margin: 0; }
-
-  /* SECTIONS */
-  section { margin-bottom: 80px; }
-  .sec-hd { display: flex; align-items: baseline; justify-content: space-between; border-bottom: 2px solid var(--ink); padding-bottom: 10px; margin-bottom: 32px; }
-  .sec-hd h2 { font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif; font-weight: 900; font-style: italic; font-size: 48px; line-height: 1; margin: 0; }
-  .sec-hd h2 em { color: var(--red); font-style: italic; }
-  .sec-hd .num { font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.14em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--muted); }
-  .intro { font-size: 18px; line-height: 1.6; max-width: 820px; margin-bottom: 32px; }
-
-  /* CODE */
-  code { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 13px; background: var(--soft); padding: 2px 7px; border-radius: 2px; }
-  pre { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 13px; line-height: 1.6; background: var(--ink); color: var(--paper); padding: 22px 24px; overflow-x: auto; margin: 16px 0 24px; border: 0; }
-  pre .c { color: rgba(255,255,255,0.35); }
-  pre .k { color: var(--acid); }
-  pre .s { color: #ff9d6e; }
-  pre .n { color: #a0d8ff; }
-  pre .v { color: #b3ff9d; }
-
-  /* CALLOUT */
-  .callout { background: var(--ink); color: var(--paper); padding: 28px 32px; margin: 28px 0; border-left: 8px solid var(--red); }
-  .callout.acid { background: var(--acid); color: var(--ink); border-color: var(--ink); }
-  .callout.red { background: var(--red); color: var(--paper); border-color: var(--ink); }
-  .callout h3 { font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif; font-weight: 900; font-style: italic; font-size: 26px; margin: 0 0 10px; }
-  .callout p { font-size: 16px; line-height: 1.5; margin: 0 0 10px; }
-  .callout p:last-child { margin: 0; }
-  .callout code { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.15); }
-  .callout.acid code { background: rgba(0,0,0,0.12); }
-
-  /* TOKENS TABLE */
-  .token-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr); gap: 14px; margin: 24px 0; }
-  .token-card { border: 2.5px solid var(--ink); overflow: hidden; box-shadow: 4px 4px 0 var(--ink); }
-  .token-swatch { height: 64px; }
-  .token-body { padding: 10px 12px; background: var(--paper); }
-  .token-name { font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; }
-  .token-val { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 11px; color: var(--muted); margin-top: 3px; }
-
-  /* TYPE SPECIMEN */
-  .type-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 28px; margin: 24px 0; }
-  .type-card { border: 2.5px solid var(--ink); padding: 22px; box-shadow: 5px 5px 0 var(--ink); }
-  .type-card .sample { line-height: 1; margin-bottom: 10px; }
-  .type-card .meta { font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.14em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--muted); padding-top: 10px; border-top: 1px solid rgba(15,15,15,0.15); }
-
-  /* ROUTE TABLE */
-  .route-table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; margin: 24px 0; }
-  .route-table th { font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.14em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 10px 14px; background: var(--ink); color: var(--paper); text-align: left; }
-  .route-table td { padding: 13px 14px; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(15,15,15,0.12); font-size: 15px; line-height: 1.4; vertical-align: top; }
-  .route-table tr:hover td { background: rgba(232,255,0,0.1); }
-  .route-table td code { font-size: 12px; }
-
-  /* HUE GRID */
-  .hue-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr); gap: 12px; margin: 24px 0; }
-  .hue-card { border: 2px solid var(--ink); overflow: hidden; }
-  .hue-swatch { height: 56px; position: relative; background-image: radial-gradient(circle at 1px 1px, rgba(0,0,0,0.85) 1px, transparent 1.4px); background-size: 5px 5px; }
-  .hue-body { padding: 10px 12px; }
-  .hue-name { font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif; font-weight: 700; font-style: italic; font-size: 16px; }
-  .hue-val { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 11px; color: var(--muted); }
-
-  /* COMPONENT LIST */
-  .comp-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 20px; margin: 24px 0; }
-  .comp-card { border: 2.5px solid var(--ink); padding: 20px 22px; box-shadow: 5px 5px 0 var(--ink); }
-  .comp-card .tag { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 12px; background: var(--ink); color: var(--acid); padding: 4px 8px; display: inline-block; margin-bottom: 10px; }
-  .comp-card h3 { font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif; font-weight: 900; font-style: italic; font-size: 22px; margin: 0 0 8px; }
-  .comp-card p { font-size: 14.5px; line-height: 1.5; margin: 0 0 8px; }
-  .comp-card .props { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 11.5px; color: var(--muted); line-height: 1.7; margin-top: 8px; padding-top: 8px; border-top: 1px dashed var(--ink); }
-
-  /* CHECKLIST */
-  .checklist { list-style: none; padding: 0; margin: 0; }
-  .checklist li { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 32px 1fr; gap: 12px; padding: 12px 0; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(15,15,15,0.12); align-items: start; font-size: 16px; line-height: 1.5; }
-  .checklist li:last-child { border-bottom: 0; }
-  .checklist .box { width: 22px; height: 22px; border: 2.5px solid var(--ink); background: var(--paper); flex-shrink: 0; margin-top: 1px; box-shadow: 2px 2px 0 var(--ink); }
-  .checklist li strong { font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif; font-weight: 700; display: block; font-size: 18px; margin-bottom: 2px; }
-
-  /* FOOTER */
-  .foot { background: var(--ink); color: var(--paper); padding: 52px 32px 28px; border-top: 6px solid var(--red); margin-top: 80px; }
-  .foot-wrap { max-width: 1200px; margin: 0 auto; }
-  .foot-big { font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif; font-weight: 900; font-style: italic; font-size: clamp(56px, 8vw, 108px); line-height: 0.85; letter-spacing: -0.02em; margin-bottom: 16px; }
-  .foot-big .comma { color: var(--red); font-style: normal; }
-  .foot-big .acid { color: var(--acid); }
-  .foot-bot { font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.14em; text-transform: uppercase; opacity: 0.45; display: flex; justify-content: space-between; padding-top: 18px; border-top: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12); }
-
-  @media (max-width: 820px) {
-    .mast { grid-template-columns: 1fr; text-align: center; gap: 10px; }
-    .mast-l, .mast-r { text-align: center; }
-    .token-grid, .hue-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; }
-    .type-grid, .comp-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
-    .wrap { padding: 0 18px 80px; }
-  }
-&lt;/style&gt;
-&lt;/head&gt;
-&lt;body&gt;
-&lt;div class="wrap"&gt;
-
-  &lt;header class="mast"&gt;
-    &lt;div class="mast-l"&gt;Developer Handoff&lt;br/&gt;&lt;span class="hot"&gt;Document · v1.0 · April 2026&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-    &lt;div class="mast-logo"&gt;Halifax&lt;span class="comma"&gt;,&lt;/span&gt; Now&lt;/div&gt;
-    &lt;div class="mast-r"&gt;For Claude Code&lt;br/&gt;&lt;span class="red"&gt;&amp; Cursor&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-  &lt;/header&gt;
-
-  &lt;section class="hero"&gt;
-    &lt;div class="kicker"&gt;★ Complete implementation spec&lt;/div&gt;
-    &lt;h1 class="title"&gt;Build this. &lt;em&gt;Right.&lt;/em&gt;&lt;/h1&gt;
-    &lt;p class="lede"&gt;Everything a developer needs to implement the Halifax Now redesign: design tokens, typography, image treatments, component specs, page routing, WordPress/Events Calendar Pro integration, and a step-by-step checklist. Read it in order. Reference it when stuck.&lt;/p&gt;
-  &lt;/section&gt;
-
-  &lt;!-- 01 OVERVIEW --&gt;
-  &lt;section&gt;
-    &lt;div class="sec-hd"&gt;&lt;h2&gt;The &lt;em&gt;Project&lt;/em&gt;&lt;/h2&gt;&lt;span class="num"&gt;§ 01&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-    &lt;p class="intro"&gt;Halifax Now is an event discovery site for Halifax, Nova Scotia. It aggregates events from multiple sources via &lt;strong&gt;The Events Calendar Pro&lt;/strong&gt; + &lt;strong&gt;Event Aggregator&lt;/strong&gt; on WordPress. The frontend is a custom theme (or headless React app) that renders events in the &lt;strong&gt;Broadsheet&lt;/strong&gt; design direction — a hybrid of editorial magazine discipline and gig-poster energy.&lt;/p&gt;
-    &lt;div class="callout acid"&gt;
-      &lt;h3&gt;The design direction.&lt;/h3&gt;
-      &lt;p&gt;The active design is called &lt;strong&gt;Broadsheet&lt;/strong&gt;. It combines:&lt;/p&gt;
-      &lt;p&gt;• &lt;strong&gt;Editorial shell&lt;/strong&gt; — Playfair Display serif masthead, Source Serif 4 body, critic's picks voice, NY Mag–style blurbs&lt;/p&gt;
-      &lt;p&gt;• &lt;strong&gt;Poster energy&lt;/strong&gt; — Anton display type for headlines, acid-yellow (#e8ff00) accents, black drop-shadows on cards, halftone image treatment, bold section banners&lt;/p&gt;
-    &lt;/div&gt;
-  &lt;/section&gt;
-
-  &lt;!-- 02 DESIGN TOKENS --&gt;
-  &lt;section&gt;
-    &lt;div class="sec-hd"&gt;&lt;h2&gt;Design &lt;em&gt;Tokens&lt;/em&gt;&lt;/h2&gt;&lt;span class="num"&gt;§ 02&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-    &lt;p class="intro"&gt;Define these as CSS custom properties on &lt;code&gt;:root&lt;/code&gt;. Use nowhere else — everything references the token.&lt;/p&gt;
-
-    &lt;div class="token-grid"&gt;
-      &lt;div class="token-card"&gt;&lt;div class="token-swatch" style="background:#f4efe6"&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="token-body"&gt;&lt;div class="token-name"&gt;Paper&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="token-val"&gt;--paper: #f4efe6&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="token-card"&gt;&lt;div class="token-swatch" style="background:#0f0f0f"&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="token-body"&gt;&lt;div class="token-name"&gt;Ink&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="token-val"&gt;--ink: #0f0f0f&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="token-card"&gt;&lt;div class="token-swatch" style="background:#c23a1e"&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="token-body"&gt;&lt;div class="token-name"&gt;Red&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="token-val"&gt;--red: #c23a1e&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="token-card"&gt;&lt;div class="token-swatch" style="background:#e8ff00; border:1px solid #ccc"&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="token-body"&gt;&lt;div class="token-name"&gt;Acid&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="token-val"&gt;--acid: #e8ff00&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="token-card"&gt;&lt;div class="token-swatch" style="background:#e8d8cc"&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="token-body"&gt;&lt;div class="token-name"&gt;Soft&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="token-val"&gt;--soft: #e8d8cc&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="token-card"&gt;&lt;div class="token-swatch" style="background:#6b6459"&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="token-body"&gt;&lt;div class="token-name"&gt;Muted&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="token-val"&gt;--muted: #6b6459&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="token-card"&gt;&lt;div class="token-swatch" style="background:#0f0f0f"&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="token-body"&gt;&lt;div class="token-name"&gt;Rule&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="token-val"&gt;--rule: #0f0f0f&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="token-card"&gt;&lt;div class="token-swatch" style="background:radial-gradient(circle at 1px 1px, rgba(0,0,0,0.045) 1px, transparent 1.2px) 0 0 / 5px 5px, #f4efe6"&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="token-body"&gt;&lt;div class="token-name"&gt;Dot BG&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="token-val"&gt;5px dot pattern&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-    &lt;/div&gt;
-
-    &lt;pre&gt;&lt;span class="c"&gt;/* CSS custom properties — add to :root */&lt;/span&gt;
-:root {
-  &lt;span class="k"&gt;--paper&lt;/span&gt;: &lt;span class="s"&gt;#f4efe6&lt;/span&gt;;   &lt;span class="c"&gt;/* warm off-white, main background */&lt;/span&gt;
-  &lt;span class="k"&gt;--ink&lt;/span&gt;: &lt;span class="s"&gt;#0f0f0f&lt;/span&gt;;     &lt;span class="c"&gt;/* near-black, text + borders */&lt;/span&gt;
-  &lt;span class="k"&gt;--red&lt;/span&gt;: &lt;span class="s"&gt;#c23a1e&lt;/span&gt;;     &lt;span class="c"&gt;/* accent red — editorial, CTAs */&lt;/span&gt;
-  &lt;span class="k"&gt;--acid&lt;/span&gt;: &lt;span class="s"&gt;#e8ff00&lt;/span&gt;;    &lt;span class="c"&gt;/* acid yellow — highlights, picks */&lt;/span&gt;
-  &lt;span class="k"&gt;--soft&lt;/span&gt;: &lt;span class="s"&gt;#e8d8cc&lt;/span&gt;;    &lt;span class="c"&gt;/* warm beige — code bg, soft fills */&lt;/span&gt;
-  &lt;span class="k"&gt;--muted&lt;/span&gt;: &lt;span class="s"&gt;#6b6459&lt;/span&gt;;  &lt;span class="c"&gt;/* warm grey — secondary text */&lt;/span&gt;
-  &lt;span class="k"&gt;--rule&lt;/span&gt;: &lt;span class="s"&gt;#0f0f0f&lt;/span&gt;;    &lt;span class="c"&gt;/* border color — same as ink */&lt;/span&gt;
-}&lt;/pre&gt;
-
-    &lt;div class="callout"&gt;
-      &lt;h3&gt;The dot pattern.&lt;/h3&gt;
-      &lt;p&gt;Every page has a subtle halftone dot texture over the background. Apply it as a &lt;code&gt;::before&lt;/code&gt; pseudo-element on &lt;code&gt;body&lt;/code&gt; or the page wrapper — &lt;code&gt;position: fixed; inset: 0; pointer-events: none; z-index: 0&lt;/code&gt; with this background:&lt;/p&gt;
-      &lt;p&gt;&lt;code&gt;radial-gradient(circle at 1px 1px, rgba(0,0,0,0.045) 1px, transparent 1.2px) 0 0 / 5px 5px&lt;/code&gt;&lt;/p&gt;
-    &lt;/div&gt;
-  &lt;/section&gt;
-
-  &lt;!-- 03 TYPOGRAPHY --&gt;
-  &lt;section&gt;
-    &lt;div class="sec-hd"&gt;&lt;h2&gt;&lt;em&gt;Typography&lt;/em&gt;&lt;/h2&gt;&lt;span class="num"&gt;§ 03&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-    &lt;p class="intro"&gt;Three font families. Load all three from Google Fonts. Never substitute with system fonts.&lt;/p&gt;
-
-    &lt;div class="type-grid"&gt;
-      &lt;div class="type-card"&gt;
-        &lt;div class="sample" style="font-family:'Playfair Display',serif; font-weight:900; font-style:italic; font-size:52px;"&gt;Playfair&lt;/div&gt;
-        &lt;div class="meta"&gt;Playfair Display · 700, 900 · Italic&lt;br/&gt;Display headings, section titles, masthead logo, pullquotes, drop-caps, event titles&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="type-card"&gt;
-        &lt;div class="sample" style="font-family:'Anton',sans-serif; font-size:52px;"&gt;ANTON&lt;/div&gt;
-        &lt;div class="meta"&gt;Anton · 400 only · All-caps&lt;br/&gt;Hero headlines, CTA buttons, page titles, the big "DO STUFF. HAVE FUN." moments&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="type-card"&gt;
-        &lt;div class="sample" style="font-family:'Source Serif 4',serif; font-size:24px; line-height:1.3;"&gt;Source Serif 4 — body text reads here, editorial voice.&lt;/div&gt;
-        &lt;div class="meta"&gt;Source Serif 4 · 400, 700&lt;br/&gt;Body copy, blurbs, lede paragraphs, descriptions. The reading voice.&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="type-card"&gt;
-        &lt;div class="sample" style="font-family:'Space Grotesk',sans-serif; font-weight:700; font-size:13px; letter-spacing:0.14em; text-transform:uppercase;"&gt;SPACE GROTESK LABELS&lt;/div&gt;
-        &lt;div class="meta"&gt;Space Grotesk · 400, 500, 700&lt;br/&gt;UI labels, chips, category tags, nav, metadata, kickers. Always uppercase + tracked when used for labels.&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;/div&gt;
-    &lt;/div&gt;
-
-    &lt;pre&gt;&lt;span class="c"&gt;/* Google Fonts import */&lt;/span&gt;
-@import url(&lt;span class="s"&gt;'https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Playfair+Display:ital,wght@0,700;0,900;1,700;1,900&amp;family=Source+Serif+4:ital,wght@0,400;0,700;1,400&amp;family=Anton&amp;family=Space+Grotesk:wght@400;500;700&amp;family=JetBrains+Mono:wght@400;500&amp;display=swap'&lt;/span&gt;);
-
-&lt;span class="c"&gt;/* Usage hierarchy */&lt;/span&gt;
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;h1&lt;/span&gt;, &lt;span class="n"&gt;.headline&lt;/span&gt;        { font-family: &lt;span class="s"&gt;'Anton'&lt;/span&gt;, sans-serif; text-transform: uppercase; }
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;h2&lt;/span&gt;, &lt;span class="n"&gt;.section-title&lt;/span&gt;   { font-family: &lt;span class="s"&gt;'Playfair Display'&lt;/span&gt;, serif; font-weight: 900; font-style: italic; }
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;h3&lt;/span&gt;, &lt;span class="n"&gt;.event-title&lt;/span&gt;     { font-family: &lt;span class="s"&gt;'Playfair Display'&lt;/span&gt;, serif; font-weight: 700; }
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;body&lt;/span&gt;, &lt;span class="n"&gt;.blurb&lt;/span&gt;         { font-family: &lt;span class="s"&gt;'Source Serif 4'&lt;/span&gt;, serif; }
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;.label&lt;/span&gt;, &lt;span class="n"&gt;.tag&lt;/span&gt;, nav   { font-family: &lt;span class="s"&gt;'Space Grotesk'&lt;/span&gt;, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.14em; text-transform: uppercase; }
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;code&lt;/span&gt;, &lt;span class="n"&gt;.mono&lt;/span&gt;          { font-family: &lt;span class="s"&gt;'JetBrains Mono'&lt;/span&gt;, monospace; }&lt;/pre&gt;
-  &lt;/section&gt;
-
-  &lt;!-- 04 IMAGE TREATMENT --&gt;
-  &lt;section&gt;
-    &lt;div class="sec-hd"&gt;&lt;h2&gt;Image &lt;em&gt;Treatments&lt;/em&gt;&lt;/h2&gt;&lt;span class="num"&gt;§ 04&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-    &lt;p class="intro"&gt;Every event image is normalized through a CSS filter + blend-mode stack. Submitters upload anything — the treatment makes it look like it belongs. No server-side processing. No canvas. Pure CSS layers.&lt;/p&gt;
-
-    &lt;div class="callout acid"&gt;
-      &lt;h3&gt;The core principle.&lt;/h3&gt;
-      &lt;p&gt;Image sits as &lt;code&gt;background-image&lt;/code&gt; on a positioned container. Two absolutely-positioned overlay &lt;code&gt;div&lt;/code&gt;s stack on top with &lt;code&gt;mix-blend-mode&lt;/code&gt;. The &lt;strong&gt;hue value&lt;/strong&gt; (0–360) comes from the event's primary category and is the only variable per event.&lt;/p&gt;
-    &lt;/div&gt;
-
-    &lt;h3 style="font-family:'Playfair Display',serif; font-weight:900; font-style:italic; font-size:32px; margin:32px 0 16px;"&gt;Treatment 1 — &lt;em&gt;Halftone&lt;/em&gt;&lt;/h3&gt;
-    &lt;p style="font-size:16px; margin-bottom:16px;"&gt;Used on the Broadsheet homepage, event cards, event detail hero, venue hero, map popup. This is the &lt;strong&gt;primary treatment&lt;/strong&gt;.&lt;/p&gt;
-    &lt;pre&gt;&lt;span class="c"&gt;/* Container */&lt;/span&gt;
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;.event-image&lt;/span&gt; {
-  position: relative;
-  overflow: hidden;
-  background: &lt;span class="s"&gt;#000&lt;/span&gt;;
-}
-
-&lt;span class="c"&gt;/* Layer 1: the photo — grayscale + contrast boost */&lt;/span&gt;
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;.event-image__photo&lt;/span&gt; {
-  position: absolute; inset: 0;
-  background-image: url(photo.jpg);
-  background-size: cover;
-  background-position: center;
-  filter: &lt;span class="v"&gt;grayscale(1) contrast(1.35) brightness(0.95)&lt;/span&gt;;
-}
-
-&lt;span class="c"&gt;/* Layer 2: category colour tint */&lt;/span&gt;
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;.event-image__tint&lt;/span&gt; {
-  position: absolute; inset: 0;
-  background: &lt;span class="v"&gt;oklch(62% 0.22 {HUE})&lt;/span&gt;; &lt;span class="c"&gt;/* HUE from category */&lt;/span&gt;
-  mix-blend-mode: multiply;
-}
-
-&lt;span class="c"&gt;/* Layer 3: halftone dot pattern */&lt;/span&gt;
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;.event-image__dots&lt;/span&gt; {
-  position: absolute; inset: 0;
-  background: radial-gradient(circle at 1px 1px, rgba(0,0,0,0.85) 1px, transparent 1.4px) 0 0 / 5px 5px;
-  mix-blend-mode: multiply;
-  opacity: 0.55;
-}&lt;/pre&gt;
-
-    &lt;h3 style="font-family:'Playfair Display',serif; font-weight:900; font-style:italic; font-size:32px; margin:32px 0 16px;"&gt;Treatment 2 — &lt;em&gt;Duotone&lt;/em&gt;&lt;/h3&gt;
-    &lt;p style="font-size:16px; margin-bottom:16px;"&gt;Used in the Listings/NY Mag variant. Softer, more editorial.&lt;/p&gt;
-    &lt;pre&gt;&lt;span class="c"&gt;/* Layer 1: soft sepia photo */&lt;/span&gt;
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;.event-image__photo&lt;/span&gt; {
-  filter: &lt;span class="v"&gt;grayscale(1) contrast(1.05) brightness(1.05) sepia(0.15)&lt;/span&gt;;
-  mix-blend-mode: multiply;
-}
-&lt;span class="c"&gt;/* Layer 2: warm gradient tint */&lt;/span&gt;
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;.event-image__tint&lt;/span&gt; {
-  background: linear-gradient(160deg,
-    oklch(92% 0.04 &lt;span class="v"&gt;{HUE}&lt;/span&gt;) 0%,
-    oklch(78% 0.08 &lt;span class="v"&gt;{HUE}&lt;/span&gt;) 100%
-  );
-  mix-blend-mode: multiply;
-  opacity: 0.85;
-}&lt;/pre&gt;
-
-    &lt;h3 style="font-family:'Playfair Display',serif; font-weight:900; font-style:italic; font-size:32px; margin:32px 0 16px;"&gt;Treatment 3 — &lt;em&gt;Neon&lt;/em&gt;&lt;/h3&gt;
-    &lt;p style="font-size:16px; margin-bottom:16px;"&gt;Used in the Tonight Board variant. Dark and moody.&lt;/p&gt;
-    &lt;pre&gt;&lt;span class="c"&gt;/* Layer 1: dark photo */&lt;/span&gt;
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;.event-image__photo&lt;/span&gt; { filter: &lt;span class="v"&gt;grayscale(1) contrast(1.3) brightness(0.55)&lt;/span&gt;; }
-&lt;span class="c"&gt;/* Layer 2: radial colour bloom */&lt;/span&gt;
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;.event-image__tint&lt;/span&gt; {
-  background: radial-gradient(circle at 70% 30%, oklch(65% 0.28 &lt;span class="v"&gt;{HUE}&lt;/span&gt;) 0%, transparent 55%);
-  mix-blend-mode: screen; opacity: 0.9;
-}
-&lt;span class="c"&gt;/* Layer 3: bottom vignette */&lt;/span&gt;
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;.event-image__vignette&lt;/span&gt; {
-  background: linear-gradient(180deg, transparent 40%, rgba(0,0,0,0.6) 100%);
-}&lt;/pre&gt;
-
-    &lt;div class="callout"&gt;
-      &lt;h3&gt;The hue table.&lt;/h3&gt;
-      &lt;p&gt;The HUE value is set server-side from the event's primary category slug. Store it on the event post or compute it in the REST API response.&lt;/p&gt;
-    &lt;/div&gt;
-
-    &lt;div class="hue-grid"&gt;
-      &lt;div class="hue-card"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-swatch" style="background-color: oklch(62% 0.22 220);"&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-body"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-name"&gt;Live Music&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-val"&gt;music · 220&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="hue-card"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-swatch" style="background-color: oklch(62% 0.22 14);"&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-body"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-name"&gt;Comedy&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-val"&gt;comedy · 14&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="hue-card"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-swatch" style="background-color: oklch(62% 0.22 280);"&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-body"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-name"&gt;Arts &amp; Culture&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-val"&gt;arts · 280&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="hue-card"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-swatch" style="background-color: oklch(62% 0.22 40);"&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-body"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-name"&gt;Food &amp; Drink&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-val"&gt;food · 40&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="hue-card"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-swatch" style="background-color: oklch(62% 0.22 140);"&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-body"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-name"&gt;Outdoors&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-val"&gt;outdoors · 140&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="hue-card"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-swatch" style="background-color: oklch(62% 0.22 20);"&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-body"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-name"&gt;Film&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-val"&gt;film · 20&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="hue-card"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-swatch" style="background-color: oklch(62% 0.22 340);"&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-body"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-name"&gt;Theatre&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-val"&gt;theatre · 340&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="hue-card"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-swatch" style="background-color: oklch(62% 0.22 120);"&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-body"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-name"&gt;Community&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-val"&gt;community · 120&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="hue-card"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-swatch" style="background-color: oklch(62% 0.22 0);"&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-body"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-name"&gt;Sports&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-val"&gt;sports · 0&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="hue-card"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-swatch" style="background-color: oklch(62% 0.22 190);"&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-body"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-name"&gt;Family&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-val"&gt;family · 190&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="hue-card"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-swatch" style="background-color: oklch(62% 0.22 300);"&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-body"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-name"&gt;Nightlife&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-val"&gt;nightlife · 300&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="hue-card"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-swatch" style="background-color: oklch(62% 0.22 90);"&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-body"&gt;&lt;div class="hue-name"&gt;Markets&lt;/div&gt;&lt;div class="hue-val"&gt;market · 90&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-    &lt;/div&gt;
-  &lt;/section&gt;
-
-  &lt;!-- 05 SHADOW BOXES --&gt;
-  &lt;section&gt;
-    &lt;div class="sec-hd"&gt;&lt;h2&gt;Shadow &lt;em&gt;Boxes&lt;/em&gt;&lt;/h2&gt;&lt;span class="num"&gt;§ 05&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-    &lt;p class="intro"&gt;Cards, buttons, and containers use an offset drop shadow — not a CSS &lt;code&gt;box-shadow&lt;/code&gt; blur, but a hard offset using a solid color. This is the poster/print-inspired detail that makes the design feel physical.&lt;/p&gt;
-    &lt;pre&gt;&lt;span class="c"&gt;/* Standard card */&lt;/span&gt;
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;.card&lt;/span&gt; {
-  border: 2.5px solid var(--ink);
-  box-shadow: 5px 5px 0 var(--ink);  &lt;span class="c"&gt;/* hard offset, no blur */&lt;/span&gt;
-  background: var(--paper);
-}
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;.card&lt;/span&gt;:hover {
-  transform: translate(-2px, -2px);
-  box-shadow: 7px 7px 0 var(--ink);  &lt;span class="c"&gt;/* offset increases on hover */&lt;/span&gt;
-}
-
-&lt;span class="c"&gt;/* CTA button */&lt;/span&gt;
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;.cta-btn&lt;/span&gt; {
-  border: 3px solid var(--ink);
-  box-shadow: 6px 6px 0 var(--ink);
-  background: var(--red); color: var(--paper);
-}
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;.cta-btn&lt;/span&gt;:hover {
-  transform: translate(-2px, -2px);
-  box-shadow: 9px 9px 0 var(--ink);
-}
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;.cta-btn&lt;/span&gt;:active {
-  transform: translate(4px, 4px);
-  box-shadow: 0 0 0 var(--ink);  &lt;span class="c"&gt;/* presses in on click */&lt;/span&gt;
-}
-
-&lt;span class="c"&gt;/* Small chip / tag */&lt;/span&gt;
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;.chip&lt;/span&gt; {
-  border: 2px solid var(--ink);
-  box-shadow: 2px 2px 0 var(--ink);
-}
-&lt;span class="n"&gt;.chip.on&lt;/span&gt; {
-  transform: translate(2px, 2px);
-  box-shadow: 0 0 0 var(--ink);  &lt;span class="c"&gt;/* selected state depresses */&lt;/span&gt;
-}&lt;/pre&gt;
-  &lt;/section&gt;
-
-  &lt;!-- 06 COMPONENTS --&gt;
-  &lt;section&gt;
-    &lt;div class="sec-hd"&gt;&lt;h2&gt;&lt;em&gt;Components&lt;/em&gt;&lt;/h2&gt;&lt;span class="num"&gt;§ 06&lt;/span&gt;&lt;/div&gt;
-    &lt;p class="intro"&gt;Build these as reusable components. Every page is composed from them.&lt;/p&gt;
-    &lt;div class="comp-grid"&gt;
-      &lt;div class="comp-card"&gt;
-        &lt;span class="tag"&gt;&amp;lt;EventCard /&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt;
-        &lt;h3&gt;Event Card&lt;/h3&gt;
-        &lt;p&gt;Poster-style card with halftone image, category kicker, Playfair title, venue/time meta, price tag. Used in Wall grid and Browse grid.&lt;/p&gt;
-        &lt;div class="props"&gt;props: event, onClick, variant?&lt;br/&gt;variants: wide (spans 2 cols), red, acid, ink&lt;br/&gt;Image: 16/10 aspect ratio, halftone treatment&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="comp-card"&gt;
-        &lt;span class="tag"&gt;&amp;lt;EventListItem /&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt;
-        &lt;h3&gt;Event List Row&lt;/h3&gt;
-        &lt;p&gt;Date box (ink bg) + category kicker + Playfair title + blurb snippet + meta + price tag. Used in Browse list view and sidebar.&lt;/p&gt;
-        &lt;div class="props"&gt;props: event, onClick&lt;br/&gt;Date box: ink bg, white text, shadow box&lt;br/&gt;Price tag: acid (free) or red (paid)&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="comp-card"&gt;
-        &lt;span class="tag"&gt;&amp;lt;Masthead /&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt;
-        &lt;h3&gt;Masthead&lt;/h3&gt;
-        &lt;p&gt;3-column header: rotated date-stamp left, "Halifax, Now" Playfair italic centre, tag right. Double-rule bottom border.&lt;/p&gt;
-        &lt;div class="props"&gt;props: date, issue, tagline&lt;br/&gt;Logo: Playfair 900 italic, red comma&lt;br/&gt;Border: 3px double var(--rule)&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="comp-card"&gt;
-        &lt;span class="tag"&gt;&amp;lt;Nav /&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt;
-        &lt;h3&gt;Navigation Bar&lt;/h3&gt;
-        &lt;p&gt;Full-width ink bar, Space Grotesk all-caps links with pipe separators. "+ Submit" CTA in acid-yellow on far right.&lt;/p&gt;
-        &lt;div class="props"&gt;props: currentPage, onNavigate&lt;br/&gt;Hover: red background on link&lt;br/&gt;CTA: acid bg, transitions to red on hover&lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;/div&gt;
-      &lt;div class="comp-card"&gt;
-        &lt;span class="tag"&gt;&amp;lt;FilterBand /&amp;gt;&lt;/span&gt;
